serving.natural_language_portion: One Hokkaido cream puff (approximately 60 g) is a single-serve pastry sold individually at Kazo Premium in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cream puff (choux pastry with cream filling) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; supplemented with HPB Singapore nutrient reference for cream-filled pastry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry for 'Hokkaido cream puff' exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for cream puff / choux pastry with custard or whipped cream filling (FDC ID 172765 and related entries) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for cream-filled pastry. Hokkaido-style puffs use a higher-fat Hokkaido milk cream, so fat and energy values are adjusted upward by approximately 10–15% relative to standard cream puff references. All values are per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of choux pastry and cream-filled pastry products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cream puff and similar wheat-flour-based pastry with dairy cream filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hokkaido cream puff. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entries for cream puff and similar choux-based pastry products (GI range 50–60 reported for cream-filled choux pastry). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 17.1) / 100 ≈ 9.4, where carbohydrate per 60 g serving = 28.5 × 0.60 = 17.1 g. GI of 55 is classified as 'low' per standard glycemic index categories (low <= 55, medium 56-69, high >= 70).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~69 mg per piece)
hypertension.guidance: A single Hokkaido cream puff contributes approximately 69 mg sodium per serving, which is low relative to the 2,000 mg daily limit; individuals with hypertension may consume it occasionally but should monitor total daily sodium from other sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (55) but refined carbohydrate content with added sugar (~14 g sugar per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 55 (low) and approximately 17 g of carbohydrate per serving (including ~8.4 g sugar), individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to one piece per occasion and consider pairing with a low-GI meal to manage postprandial glucose response, despite its low GI classification.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~10.8 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~115 mg per 100 g) from Hokkaido dairy cream
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content from Hokkaido milk cream (~6.5 g per serving) exceeds one-third of the recommended daily saturated fat limit; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict consumption to occasional treats and prioritise lower-fat alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kazu Kazu Hokkaido Cream Puff is a Japanese-inspired choux pastry dessert sold in Singapore, notably at Kazo Premium (Funan Mall). The pastry shell is made from classic choux dough — a cooked paste of butter, water, flour, and eggs — baked until hollow and crisp on the outside. It is filled with a rich, lightly sweetened whipped cream made using Hokkaido milk, a premium dairy product from Hokkaido prefecture in northern Japan, renowned for its high fat content and naturally sweet, clean flavour. The result is a soft, airy shell with a cold, pillowy cream interior. Each piece weighs approximately 60 g and is sold individually at around SGD 5.80. Nutritionally, the puff is energy-dense at approximately 298 kcal per 100 g, with notable saturated fat (~10.8 g/100 g) and sugar (~14 g/100 g) from the dairy cream filling. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), making it a low glycemic food. Clinically, it is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its refined carbohydrate and sugar content, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat load. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for those with these allergies. The dish reflects the broader Singapore trend of Japanese-inspired premium desserts and is popular as a snack or light dessert.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Hokkaido cream puff exists in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA cream puff / choux pastry entries with upward adjustment for Hokkaido high-fat dairy cream. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar cream-filled choux pastry. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central cream puff / choux pastry entries (FDC reference range) with a 10–15% upward adjustment to fat and energy to reflect the higher-fat Hokkaido milk cream used in this product. 2. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cream-filled choux pastry; no direct measurement for Hokkaido cream puff was found. GI of 55 is classified as 'low' per standard glycemic index categories (low <= 55, medium 56-69, high >= 70). 3. Serving size of 60 g is estimated based on typical single-serve cream puff weight for premium Japanese-style puffs sold in Singapore; actual weight may vary by vendor. 4. Halal status is listed as false as Kazo Premium's halal certification status could not be confirmed from approved sources; this should be verified with the restaurant directly. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA cream puff composite data and are not independently confirmed for this specific Hokkaido product. 6. No core macro is zero; all four core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a cream-filled choux pastry.
